Built from years inside founder-led businesses

01 / The Founder Brand Gap

The problem

You're more impressive in person than you appear online, and that gap is costing you.

An investor deciding whether to take your meeting looked you up online this week. So did the senior hire who could transform your next 12 months, and the partner deciding between you and a competitor. They found plenty about your product, but very little about the person building it. So they formed a view anyway.

The cost is real. The founder whose stories investors have followed for months walks in with momentum, while the one still building conviction inside the room fights a disadvantage no deck overcomes. Given the choice, the best investors back the founder they already understand. One of them had already seen your stories and decided their ticket size before even hearing your pitch.

And it compounds. Without publishing your thinking online, every investor, hire and partner has to be won in person, one conversation at a time, so you're pulled off the business and the growth that would make raising easier slows. A founder brand breaks that cycle: the market arrives already trusting the way you think, and you stay focused on building.

You've got years of stories worth telling.

You just need the space to think, and a team to bring them to life.

Image credit: The Nook by Isaac French

The market shift

Trust has moved from the brand to the founder.

The way businesses earn trust has changed for good, and most founders are still running the old marketing playbook. Paid advertising still works, but it's becoming too expensive to rely on. A trusted founder now does what brand advertising used to, without the cost, earning the same attention and demand at a fraction of the price, turning their stories into sales.

You don't need to be a professional writer, you need to be a storyteller.

Old marketing playbook
The new default
Corporate brand trust
Founder trust
Paid ads drove acquisition
Founder stories drive sales
Brand-led distribution
Founder-brand publishing
Polished brand messaging
Real founder perspective

The fundraising shift

Raising has changed too.

Capital is more concentrated and more selective than it was, rounds take longer, and investors set a higher bar on traction and credibility before they'll engage. The founders getting funded are the ones the market already knows and trusts, the ones whose thinking investors have followed long before the pitch. A visible founder brand has gone from an edge to what gets you in the room.

What a founder brand is worth

44%
of a company's market value is directly tied to the reputation of the founder behind it.
Weber Shandwick, The CEO Reputation Premium
82%
of consumers say they trust a brand more when the founder is visible and authentic
Edelman Trust Barometer
89%
of decision-makers trust founders who show consistent thought leadership over time
Weber Shandwick
30–50%
higher valuation multiples for companies led by founders with strong visible presence
Weber Shandwick

The missing half

A business brand without a founder brand is only half-built.

You're responsible for two brands: the company and the founder behind it. Most founders pour everything into the company, the product and their story. The result is a business people recognise, led by a founder they don't. Today, the two are inseparable. People back, buy from and join founders by following their journey online.

To be WellBuilt. is to build both: a company the market understands, and a founder the market trusts.

The business brand
The founder brand
What you sell
How you think online
The product or service
The judgement behind it
The company story
The thinking only you have
Earns attention
Earns trust
Tells the market what you do
Tells the market how you think

Why this matters now

AI can fake expertise. It can't fake your stories.

AI has made it easy to produce content that sounds smart but says nothing. Anyone can generate it now, so it carries no weight without the founder behind it. What it can't produce is the experience you're living through, and that's what the market trusts, because it's real.

The most valuable asset a founder can own over the next 20 years is the one thing AI can't fake: clear thinking, told as stories until the right people know you for it.

Writing isn't the bottleneck anymore. Thinking clearly enough to tell a story worth publishing is.

02 / Who You Become

Extroverted in real life and introverted online

The cost is real, but it ends the moment you become the founder the market can see.

The most trusted founders online are rarely the loudest.

You don't have to become an influencer

A founder brand isn't a personal brand.

A personal brand is becoming known as a person, the influencer who turns their own life into content and themselves into the product. That's what most founders reject, and they're right to. It isn't who they are, or why they started a business.

A founder brand asks none of that. You don't have to be famous, perform, or share anything private. You're still a builder, talking about what you're building.

Your thinking and stories become visible to the people who need to see them: your judgement, your decisions and the way you see your market. What changes is that you grow into the public founder your business now needs.

A creator builds an audience. A founder builds a company.

A creator's content is the business. A founder's content supports it.

A creator optimises for
A founder optimises for
Views
Investor confidence
Engagement
Talent attraction
Algorithms
Customer trust
Audience growth
Strategic partnerships
Consistency for its own sake
Long-term reputation

I don't believe founders need to become influencers. But businesses become easier to trust when the stories behind them are visible. And ultimately, it's easier to make money when people know you.

Papillon, Founder of WellBuilt.

Who you need to be

An iconic founder, building in public.

The one the market remembers. Known for how they think, trusted before the meeting starts, the name that comes up when the round, the hire or the deal is being decided. That's who you become.

Future you is worth more than you are today.

Your thinking, your network and your body of work grow with time. You're building something that keeps working for you long after it's published.

You share your thinking because the founder who made it didn't keep it to themselves. Keep going, and being seen becomes as natural as running the company.

The founders publishing their stories online now are building fundraising, hiring and commercial advantages that get harder to overtake the longer you wait.

The long game

A reputation that compounds while you build.

Nobody goes to the gym for 30 days and expects to stay strong for life. Building your reputation in public is the same, a body of work that compounds as you build the company. The founders who get known for their thinking keep showing up for it, week on week.

Within 30 days
You’ve gained clarity of thought, and the criticism you feared never comes. Telling your stories quickly becomes enjoyable.
Within six months
It’s now second nature, and the right people are paying attention. You’ll walk into meetings where people mention your latest story, already warmer to you before it begins.
Year by year
Your stories become a permanent record of the founder you're becoming as you scale your company. Your name now carries weight, the deals, hires and investors come to you.

Imagine walking into a room where you're trusted before you've said a word. The investor already knows how you think. The customer has already decided to buy. The hire already wants to work with you.

This happens when the market has read your stories for months before you ever met.

The promise you make to yourself

There's a future version of you who made it.

Whether you become them depends on whether the market ever sees it. So you keep telling your stories, and one day you look back at a year of building in the open and know your future self made it. Not because every story was polished, but because you kept telling the true one.

03 / Who It's For

We work with a specific kind of founder

You’re a Seed, Series A or growth-stage tech founder. Your company brand is built and working. You’re the half of it the market can’t see.

You’ve built something complex, but the thinking behind it lives in your head. The market can’t see your conviction, your experience or the ideas driving your company. We extract that thinking, build the system around it, and turn it into a public body of work that sounds like you, making your expertise as visible as the company you’ve built.

WellBuilt. isn’t for you
WellBuilt. is for you
✕Looking for someone to post generic AI content on your behalf
✓You have years of experience and ideas but little time to turn them into stories
✕Want to chase followers, virality or personal fame
✓You want to be the founder your market names first

✓You want your reputation to compound while you stay focused on building the business

✓You want a long-term strategic partner

04 / The Two Halves Intensive

Two things must be true

You have to think clearly about what you're building, and share it online where the market can see it.

Most founders are missing one or the other, so they stay invisible.

The Two Halves Intensive

Turn your thinking into your reputation.

We ask the questions that make you think differently about the company you’re building. The answers are yours, sharper than the version in your head, and once they’re captured they become a body of work across LinkedIn, your newsletter and, in time, your book.

The hardest part of sharing your stories online is making someone stop scrolling and take notice.

05 / Inside The Two Halves

The Two Halves Intensive · Four weeks

Every founder starts here.

The strategic foundation behind everything you'll publish.

£5,995* / $7,999

What's included

✓Founder & Company Brand Alignment, so your founder and company strengthen each other
✓Story Extraction Sessions, so the thinking you’ve never articulated becomes stories your market can understand
✓Your Endless Story Bank, so you start noticing the decisions and conversations that show how you think
✓WellBuilt. Voice Playbook, so everything published in your name sounds unmistakably like you
✓Publishing-Ready LinkedIn Profile, so the right people understand what you’re building before they read a single post

*VAT added in the UK.

Where it begins

A company the market understands, and a founder the market trusts.

Most businesses never connect the two on purpose, so the founder behind the company is rarely understood. We build that connection deliberately: where the two stories overlap, where they stay distinct, and how each one makes the other stronger.

Before we surface a single story, we decide what your founder brand stands for and how it strengthens the company you've already built. This is the groundwork the rest of the four weeks depends on.

Next we find your stories, then your voice.

With the two brands aligned, we surface the raw material, the experiences only you have lived. Then your Voice Playbook shapes how they get told: the themes you return to, the way you think, and the standard anything published in your name has to meet.

The stories are already yours. The Playbook makes sure they always sound like you.

After the Intensive

Continue building both halves.

The Intensive builds the foundation. From there, we write your LinkedIn and your weekly newsletter. After twelve months, that body of work can become a book.

Available to Intensive clients only.

Depth over volume

Every story gets the care your thinking deserves.

A founder’s reputation is built over time. Crafting their public body of work across LinkedIn, newsletters and annual books isn’t the work of an agency with junior writers and hundreds of clients. It’s close work. It takes the emotional intelligence to draw out what you actually think, the curiosity to uncover the stories behind it, the storytelling to make the market want to read it, and the writing skills to capture and keep attention online. There’s a limit to how many founders we can do that for properly.

We work with three to five founders at a time.

What the market already pays

The best tech companies pay six figures for this.

$320k
PayPal
Full-time CEO writer
Per year
$236k
Anthropic
Full-time human writer
Per year

Applications for PayPal’s role closed in 36 hours, and Anthropic explicitly required writing samples produced without AI. The companies closest to AI are the ones insisting a human represents how their leaders think.

That’s what the market pays to make founder thinking visible. The Two Halves Intensive is where yours begins.

06 / Why WellBuilt.

Why WellBuilt.

I've built the brands, raised the money and written the stories.

Along the way, I’ve learned that the founders people remember are the ones they understand. That’s the idea behind WellBuilt.

Thirty years of translating complexity into clarity

My career has taken me from hedge funds and investment banking to interviewing CEOs and telling stories for some of the world’s leading five-star hotels, private aviation companies and luxury brands. I’ve also built, fundraised and scaled my own brands across wellness, hospitality and consumer products, including launches into NET-A-PORTER, MR PORTER and Selfridges.

The industries may be different, but the challenge is the same: making something complicated easy to understand without losing what makes it valuable. Investors, customers and future hires back what they understand. I keep asking what something means until I understand it, and if I understand it, your market will too.

What raising taught me

I raised £500k for one of my brands and it wasted months, speaking to over a hundred investors who weren't the right fit before finding the few who were, repeating myself in meeting after meeting. It taught me just how much a founder's own reputation carries the raise.

What the work proved

More recently I've worked 1:1 with founders and CEOs under NDA, writing their stories while publishing my own thinking online. Without exception, the founders who told their stories were easier to trust and remember, and harder to compete against.

The founders attracting disproportionate trust, demand and opportunity aren’t necessarily better than you, they’ve simply made their stories easier to discover.

Edwin Brenninkmeyer

What changes when founder thinking becomes visible

"We felt from the start we were in excellent hands. I cannot recommend Papillon highly enough."

From the outset, Papillon asked the right questions and quickly understood our business. Her energy, attention to detail and ability to bring new ideas we hadn't considered made a meaningful difference. Her level of preparation, clarity and follow-through built real trust.

Founder and CEO, Oriens Aviation
